title: racosperma subulatum
intro: Racosperma subulatum, also known as the "Desert Myall" or "Hickory Wattle", is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a small to medium-sized shrub that is native to the arid regions of Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. It has been prepared in herbal forms such as tea and tincture, and is said to provide health benefits such as relief from fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
